# Rule 25 — Auto-generated docs must start with `<!-- AUTO-GENERATED -->` marker

> **Category:** `lint-enforced-convention`
> **Severity:** `blocker`
> **Last validated:** 2026-05-15 by @Skords-01
> **Next review:** 2026-08-13
> **Status:** Active

> Per-rule canonical body for Hard Rule #25. Compact summary lives in [`AGENTS.md § Hard rules`](../../../AGENTS.md#hard-rules-do-not-break). The machine-readable registry lives in [`docs/governance/hard-rules.json`](../hard-rules.json). 3-way sync (AGENTS.md ↔ JSON ↔ this file) is enforced by `pnpm lint:hard-rules-registry`.

## Scope

Every auto-generated artifact in the repo. Currently:

- `docs/governance/knowledge-graph.html`
- `docs/governance/symbol-index.html`
- `docs/02-engineering/architecture/diagrams/c3-workspaces.md`
- `docs/open-work.md`
- `docs/initiatives/follow-ups.md`
- `docs/00-start/playbooks/INDEX.md`
- `docs/governance/hard-rules-matrix.md`

Add new artifacts here when a new generator lands.

## Enforced by

- **convention** — every generator's render pipeline emits the marker as part of the deterministic output; the matching `--check` flag re-renders the file in memory and fails on disk diff. There is no separate marker-only linter — the diff check covers the marker implicitly because the marker is part of the canonical output. If the marker is stripped, the file diverges from the generated bytes and `--check` exits 1.

## Why / What is enforced

Auto-generated files are reviewed differently from hand-maintained ones: reviewers should skip them when judging editorial value (they're a mirror, not the source of truth), and bots / scripts should know not to hand-edit them.

The `<!-- AUTO-GENERATED -->` marker is the visible signal in plain text. Markers are placed at the start of the body (after the freshness header so Rule #10 still applies) with the regeneration command — example:

```markdown
> **Last validated:** YYYY-MM-DD by @handle. **Next review:** YYYY-MM-DD.
> **Status:** Active

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE. Do not edit by hand. Regenerate via `pnpm docs:gen-…`. -->
```

For HTML dashboards the marker is an HTML comment block at the top of `<body>`.

For JSON outputs (e.g. `knowledge-graph.json`, `repo-map.auto.json`), JSON has no comments — instead the `$schema` field points at the canonical schema and the schema's `description` documents that the artifact is auto-generated. The `--check` mode of each generator catches hand edits the same way (diff fail).

## Tracking

- Initiative — [`docs/initiatives/archive/_0014-knowledge-graph-and-catalogs.md`](../../initiatives/archive/_0014-knowledge-graph-and-catalogs.md).
- ADR-0058 — [`docs/adr/0058-knowledge-graph-schema.md`](../../adr/0058-knowledge-graph-schema.md) (schema-first principle).
